what_it_does: TypeScript declaration for ContainmentTree — the block SOLVER. Declares ContainmentTreeProps (node: CVNode, plus three axis dials lod/surface/density and an injectable DiagramSolver) and the ContainmentTree function, whose return type augments JSX.Element with registerAtom(role, fn) and atomRoles() methods.
observations: Imports CVNode type from ./cv-nodes (the shared node type)

Three named axes typed as string unions: lod ('summary'|'pitch'|'full'), surface ('desktop'|'tablet'|'mobile-portrait'|'mobile-landscape'|'slide'|'print'|'web'), density ('compact'|'comfortable'|'spacious')

JSDoc per prop documents intent: lod = content zoom (prunes/grows tree, independent of surface); surface = screen/ratio (reflow + locked band aspect); density = breathing room (scales spacing at every level)
DiagramSolver typed as `any` — injected to render kind:'diagram' nodes (pass window.<NS>.DiagramSolver)

Component docstring: 'THE BLOCK SOLVER' — renders a CVNode tree as the containment ladder; zone wash computed from depth; LOD prunes before layout; same node at different lod/surface/density computes a different concrete design
Return type augments JSX.Element with registerAtom (add atom role without editing the solver — 'atoms = data') and atomRoles() (list registered roles)
standouts: The function's return type is declared as JSX.Element WITH extra methods (registerAtom/atomRoles) — typing the component as also being an extension point / registry handle
'atoms = data' — atom roles are registered, not coded into the solver
Explicit statement that the same node at different axis values 'computes a different concrete design' — the generative-design thesis encoded in the type docs
conventions: Axis dials typed as string-literal unions (the DS dial convention)
Dependency injection of the sibling solver (DiagramSolver) rather than hard import
concerns: DiagramSolver typed as `any` loses type safety at the solver-to-solver seam
notes: Type contract for THE block solver: takes a CVNode tree + three axis dials, renders the containment ladder, and exposes registerAtom/atomRoles so atom roles are registrable data not code.
questions: Why is DiagramSolver `any` rather than a typed interface — is the graph solver's type intentionally decoupled?
How does a JSX.Element return value also carry callable methods (registerAtom/atomRoles) at runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — declaration file; the JSDoc 'block solver / generative' purpose matches the declared props and extension API.
intention_signals: Trying to type a generative layout engine where one content tree plus orthogonal dials yields different concrete designs, and where new leaf types are added by registration rather than by editing the solver.
novelty: Typing a React component's return as JSX.Element augmented with a registration API (registerAtom/atomRoles), making the component itself an extension point.
contribution: Contributes the typed public contract for the block solver — the lod/surface/density axis vocabulary, the CVNode input, the injectable DiagramSolver seam, and the registerAtom extension API a fused system would keep.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript declaration for the ContainmentTree block solver: CVNode input, lod/surface/density axis dials, injectable DiagramSolver, and a registerAtom/atomRoles extension API.
intention_for_embedding: To type a generative layout engine that renders a content tree into the containment ladder differently per axis dial, extensible via registered atom roles.
